title: LM_LOGFILE
text: LM_LOGFILE This option displays various notification messages in the log file, starting from the NumberOfDays before the license expiry date. LM_LOGFILE = NumberOfDays | true | false NumberOfDays — any value from 0 (license expiration text is never displayed) through to 100 . True/false — log all/no license notifications in the log file. If omitted or set to a value less than 0 , the default value of LM_TRANSCRIPT is used. If LM_TRANSCRIPT is used, that will apply to all types of messages. But if not, the default will be current 30 days for all messages except for the maintenance support notifications. For maintenance support notifications, it will be set as 0, that is it will be off. This will cause the maintenance support warnings to not appear by default. They will only appear if LM_MAINTENANCE_NOTIFICATION is set. If set to a value greater than 100 , the maximum value of 100 is used. Any other value generates an error message and informs the user that the value will be set to the default value. LM_LOGFILE also accepts On and Off, true and false. On/true defaults to 30 days, Off/false is the same as 0. If not specified it defaults to what is specified on LM_TRANSCRIPT .
